prompt:
HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. Good luck!

You are an expert CEFR writing assessor.
Assess the student text using the provided task/context blocks as supporting evidence.
Use EXPERT_ANNOTATION, CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, and RANKING_ANCHORS only as supporting context; never override direct evidence from the student text.
Return ONLY one CEFR label from: A2|B1|B2.
Do not output JSON.
Do not output explanation.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. Good luck!
TEXT_TYPE: essay

I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Actually, I think it's not bad at all. It means that you use all your resources to get better. I chose the same way of studying, I try to get high marks and so I almost don't have any free time either. But I believe this is the right decition. However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. I can give you some tips how to get it. Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. For example, I like to do a lot of assignments on weekends, so each my day gets easier. But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. However, some people prefer to have fully free weekends. So you should try both and decide which option is better for you. Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. But very important thing: don't abuse this help. You should not care that much about this. Everyone got same time a day and most people study good with it.
To get more free time you should organise your whole week. Write down all your deadlines to be done and all homeworks you want to start. Allocate time for tasks per day, and you'll see how fast you're getting everyday tasks done.

Also Its important to understand what you actually need to do. If subject seems not intresting and its not needed in next courses and for your life, why do you need to do all the tasks and get exhausted every day to get max grade?

Tip number one. Try to rewrite solution from other students. I agree it is bad, but if you try to understand, what your friend write and understand the main idea of solution, you can solve the same problem in future.

Tip number two. Do sport. It is not obviously tip, but it really work. When you play, for exmaple, basketball or football your brain relax and do not do anything, so after training you can solve spend less time for doing homework than before traning.

Tip number three. More time of sleeping, more energy at work. It is obviously. If you sleep aproximately three hours, of course you do not have energy for working. If you sleep more than six hours, you wake up and understand that you can do everything.

Tip number four. Do homework after getting. It is obviously too. If you spend time now for preparing, you dont spend time in future for doing this, so you have more time for preparing for exams, so you have better mark than if you sleep in first iteration.
